hypothesis: If adaptive, multi-metric diversity constraints (representation-, string-, fragment-, and scaffold-based) are applied during REINVENT4 reinforcement learning for singlet fission, then the generated molecules will exhibit significantly higher chemical diversity (lower mean pairwise similarity, more clusters, higher unique fragment/scaffold rates, fewer overrepresented n-grams) without reducing the fraction meeting the ΔE_SF objective because dynamic penalties on overrepresented patterns prevent mode collapse across complementary molecular representations.
